_notes: Human-authored 2026-07-19 (this pack never had a classify_districts.py run; authored directly). Norfolk's 2018 zoning ordinance (city-hosted at norfolkva.gov/norfolkzoningordinance) was scraped with SINGLE-ELEMENT breadcrumbs (e.g. ['3.2 Residential Base Zoning Districts']), so per app/source_classifier.py both 'article' (breadcrumb[1]) and 'division' (breadcrumb[2]) are EMPTY at runtime and rules can only key on title_contains. Fortunately every per-district section title embeds the district code and name ('3.2.5. SF-6: Single Family 6'), so rules key on the distinctive 'CODE: Name' fragment rather than numeric prefixes (a bare title_contains '3.2.' would false-match '5.3.2.'-style titles elsewhere in the ordinance). The group use tables (3.2.12 residential, 3.3.9 commercial, 3.4.11 downtown, 3.5.7 industrial) are each keyed by their unique 'Uses for the ... Base Zoning District' titles and labeled with their group's district term -- these tables carry the P/C use matrices that ground most retrieval. District identities confirmed against the regional Hampton Roads Locality_Zones service (services.arcgis.com/ex1Az95h0eiponPK/arcgis/rest/services/Locality_Zones/FeatureServer/7 'Zoning_Norfolk', field ZONE: 74 distinct codes matching the ordinance's base districts plus per-project PD variants). Mapping rationale: (1) residential-low-density: SF-2/SF-4/SF-6/SF-10/SF-T single-family, R-C Residential-Coastal, plus the all-residential collapse per lynchburg/salem/staunton precedent for MF-NS/MF-AC/MF-HR multi-family (3.2.3-3.2.11) and the 3.2.12 use table. (2) commercial-employment: C-C Community Commercial, C-R Regional Commercial, O Office, BC-O Business & Commerce Park-Office (3.3.4-3.3.7), the 3.3.9 use table, and IN/IN-C Institutional (3.7.3-3.7.4, the lynchburg IN-1/IN-2 institutional precedent). NOTE: C-N Neighborhood Commercial exists in the GIS layer and in Table 3.3.9's columns but its own section (3.3.3) is absent from this scrape -- nothing to key; the use table still covers it. (3) industrial-zone: BC-I Business & Commerce Park-Industrial (3.3.8, grouped under ch. 3.3 but industrial in kind), I-L/I-G/I-W/I-DW (3.5.3-3.5.6) and the 3.5.7 use table. (4) mixed-use-core: all six downtown base districts D-W/D-BC/D-MU/D-AD/D-FN/D-SP (3.4.5-3.4.10; multi-family and hotels by right alongside commercial per Table 3.4.11), the 3.4.11 use table, and the special-purpose corridor mixed-use districts MW Monarch Way, TOD Transit-Oriented Development, G-1 Granby/Monticello Corridor Mixed-use (3.7.9/3.7.11/3.7.12). Deliberately left UNMAPPED (fall through to unknown/general): HC-* Historic and Cultural Conservation base districts (3.6, neighborhood-specific hybrid regs), A Airport, OSP Open Space, RESOURCE, MI Military Installation, EBH East Beach Harbor (3.7 special-purpose non-development or niche districts), the heterogeneous 3.7.13 special-purpose use table, PD planned developments (3.8, chesapeake PD precedent), all 3.9 overlays, and chapters 1-2/4-9 (procedures, supplemental standards, nonconformities, enforcement, interpretation, approved-district rosters).
